drivers.label: Revenue growth (Yr 1–10)
drivers.comment: MSFT grew ~15% in FY25, led by Azure (~30%+) and Copilot/AI monetization. Each scenario fades from a Yr-1 rate to mature growth by Yr 10. Base ≈ trend; Bull assumes AI/Cloud keeps re-accelerating; Bear is conservative.

drivers.label: EBIT margin (Yr 1–10)
drivers.comment: MSFT already runs a ~45–46% operating margin — the highest of the mega-caps. Scenarios hold roughly flat to slightly expanding despite heavy AI capex. The Bull path ending near 49% would be an all-time high — plausible only if cloud/AI scale drives real operating leverage.

drivers.label: Capex (Yr 1–10, % rev)
drivers.comment: Very elevated near-term for the AI data-centre build-out (FY25 capex ran ~25%+ of revenue), then declining. That decline path is the single biggest swing factor in the whole model.

drivers.label: D&A (% of revenue)
drivers.comment: ~8% of revenue — a touch higher than peers because the data-centre build-out is now depreciating. Modeled stable; not a swing factor.

drivers.label: Net working capital (% of revenue)
drivers.comment: Now a small net drag (0–2% of revenue). MSFT carries a large unearned/deferred-revenue balance (~$60B+) that funds much of its receivables growth, so incremental working capital is roughly neutral — arguably a source of cash. The earlier 14–16% figure overstated the drag and understated value — this is the fix (the same correction applied to GOOGL).

drivers.label: Tax rate
drivers.comment: 21% US statutory; MSFT's effective rate has run ~18–19%, so this trims NOPAT a touch (biases value down).

drivers.label: Risk-free rate
drivers.comment: ≈ the 10-year Treasury yield. ~4% is consistent with current levels.

drivers.label: Equity risk premium
drivers.comment: 3.9–4.3% ≈ Damodaran's implied ERP of 4.23% (2026). This is where current market conditions legitimately enter the model.

drivers.label: Beta
drivers.comment: ~0.95–1.05; MSFT roughly tracks the market, slightly defensive in the Bull case (less perceived risk).

drivers.label: WACC (derived)
drivers.comment: CAPM build-up nets to ~7.6–8.6% across scenarios. Lower WACC in Bull compounds with higher growth to lift value.

drivers.label: Terminal growth
drivers.verdict: Base OK · Bull near the ceiling
drivers.comment: Terminal g should not exceed the ~4% risk-free / long-run nominal GDP. Bull's 3.3% sits comfortably below that edge.

drivers.label: Scenario probability
drivers.comment: Weights already lean bullish (Bull 42% / Base 45%). MSFT's premium multiple implies even more weight on the Bull path — that's the AI/cloud optimism, priced.
